message:
feat(core): add binomial_heap.rs — mergeable priority queue with worst-case O(log n) guarantees

Implements a binomial heap as a forest of binomial trees satisfying the
min-heap property. Unlike the existing Fibonacci heap (amortized bounds),
binomial heaps provide worst-case O(log n) for all operations including
merge, making them suitable for latency-sensitive scheduling where
amortized spikes are unacceptable.

Key properties:
- O(1) find-min via cached minimum pointer
- O(log n) worst-case insert, merge, extract-min
- Arena-allocated binomial trees — each tree of order k has exactly
  2^k nodes, and the heap maintains at most one tree per order
- Merge operates like binary addition with carry

579 lines implementation + 211 lines property tests covering:
- Heap ordering invariant (extract-min yields sorted sequence)
- Merge correctness (merged heap contains union of elements)
- Size tracking consistency
- Insert/extract-min roundtrip

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
